Summary

Dynamic Development & Production Planning Section Head with over 20 years of experience in the oil and gas industry. Expertise in managing annual work programmes, overseeing project budgets, and optimising production processes through rigorous cost control and performance analysis.

Proven ability to implement cross-functional strategies that increase operational efficiency — well-positioned to drive growth and innovation in development and production planning.

Years Experience

Roles at CNPC
Career

Work Experience

Feb 2009 — Present
CNPC

Development & Production Planning Section Head

  • Oversaw geological development studies and production methodologies in oil and gas fields, ensuring adherence to industry best practices.
  • Formulated the annual Work Programme and Budget (WPB) for development and production departments guided by approved KPIs, while forecasting a five-year WPB.
  • Conducted economic evaluations for new development and production projects to achieve company targets and enhance revenue.
  • Prepared and managed Authorisation for Expenditure (AFE) based on comprehensive work programmes, closely monitoring project performance.
  • Compiled Monthly Progress Reports (MPR) integrating VOWD, physical progress, payment statements, and quality control metrics.
  • Performed cost analysis employing Earned Value Management (EVM) methodologies and other analytical techniques.
  • Executed timely interventions upon forecasts indicating budget overruns, calculating Estimates at Completion (EAC).
  • Developed long-term plans evaluating CAPEX and OPEX in conjunction with Development & Production Technical Reviews (DPTR).
  • Prepared Budget Change Requests (BCR) for project cases involving deviations, additions, and cancellations.
  • Coordinated procurement processes to ensure timely delivery of materials to site, effectively managing purchase orders.
  • Analysed production data to identify optimisation opportunities and implement cost reduction measures.
  • Generated detailed daily, weekly, and monthly progress reports, highlighting critical activities to identify bottlenecks.
Jan 2007 — Feb 2009
CNPC

Process Engineer

  • Managed operation of the Distributed Control System (DCS) and the main processing train.
  • Conducted periodic checks on operational efficiency of desalters, heat exchangers, and chemical injection skids.
  • Analysed and addressed abnormal operational conditions promptly, employing sound judgement.
  • Provided technical support for crude oil processing within the Vis breaking unit.
  • Diagnosed major plant upsets and chronic operational issues, identifying improvement opportunities.
  • Conducted regular safety audits to uncover potential hazards and ensure regulatory compliance.
  • Coordinated capacity increase studies alongside operating facilities and engineering design contractors.
  • Monitored and analysed KPIs for the Vis breaking process, implementing corrective actions where necessary.
Jul 2005 — Dec 2006
CNPC

Lab Analyst

  • Managed procurement of all chemical agents required for field production operations and ensured adequate storage levels.
  • Performed standard tests and analyses on crude samples — gravity, water & sediment, salt content, vapour pressure, viscosity, flash points, pour points, and other vital parameters.
  • Evaluated demulsifiers, reverse demulsifiers, PPDs, asphaltene inhibitors, and wax inhibitors in laboratory and field settings.
  • Established and implemented quality control procedures to guarantee accuracy and reliability of laboratory results.
  • Provided training to operators on safe chemical handling practices onsite.
  • Calibrated laboratory instruments and performed routine maintenance on equipment.
